title: Emerging respiratory pathogens and Severe Acute Respiratory Infection (SARI) case report form - Canada.ca
content:
## Emerging respiratory pathogens and Severe Acute Respiratory Infection (SARI) case report form
The document is available for downloading or viewing:
Emerging respiratory pathogens and Severe Acute Respiratory Infection (SARI) case report form (PDF document - 1,529 KB - 8 pages)
Version 0.9 (Last updated May 15, 2024)
Instructions for completion
- Please complete as much detail as possible on this form at the time of the initial report.
- It is not expected that all fields will be completed during the initial report, but that updates will be made when information becomes available.
Reporting instructions for local public health authorities
- Please report cases using normal local / provincial / territorial methods.
Reporting instructions for provincial / territorial public health authorities
- Reporting to the Public Health Agency is required within 24 hours of case notification to Provincial / Territorial Public Health.
- During regular business hours (8:00 – 5:00pm ET):
- fax completed form (without first page) to 1-613-952-4723 and send an email notification (do not attach form) to erp-pre@phac-aspc.gc.ca ; OR
- contact erp - pre@phac-aspc.gc.ca to discuss alternative reporting arrangements .
- After regular business hours (8:00 – 5:00pm ET): please contact the Health Portfolio Operations Centre by phone at 1-800-545-7661 or hpoc-cops@phac-aspc.gc.ca to report the event and discuss method for information transfer.
- please contact the Health Portfolio Operations Centre by phone at 1-800-545-7661 or hpoc-cops@phac-aspc.gc.ca to report the event and discuss method for information transfer.
